summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc integration in Firefox have brought important enhancements to video handling, most notably supporting **H.264**, a widely used video compression standard essential for online streaming. The changes streamline the codebase by switching deprecated functions and utilizing more modern memory management techniques, which helps improve the reliability and efficiency of video encoding and decoding processes. Additionally, by refining how the browser assesses its media capabilities and configuring audio streams, these updates ensure better performance and adaptability during video calls, enhancing the overall user experience. _Overall, these improvements contribute to more robust real-time communication features in Firefox._
